source: Remediation sequence C1-C6 (fresh-eyes audit 2026-06-06); filed for later manual implementation. See docs/PROJECT_MAP.md and the approved plan.
title: Surface store-divergence via the replication log (debugging)
what_failed: memory_write already records per-write Supabase vs SQLite status (_replication_log_append), but nothing flags when the two stores disagree - a silent C1 divergence at the storage layer goes unnoticed.
where_failed: tools/tools_memory.py:842 _replication_log_append; no consumer checks for status mismatches.
why_likely: The log was added for forensics but never wired to an alert/report.
migration_plan: 1. A small read-only check (passive skill or verify step) scanning the replication log for insert rows where supabase_status != sqlite_status.
2. Report divergent writes (count + ids); exit non-zero in --strict.
3. Reuse the existing log - add no new storage.
implementation_notes: Smart and simple: consume an asset that already exists. Read-only, no new infra. Runtime debugging counterpart to the test-time round-trip contract.
analysis: Debugging aid: when a write half-lands (one store ok, one fails), this turns a silent divergence into a visible report.
